lines.text: AMERICAN THORACIC SOCIETY
DOCUMENTS
Diagnosis and Treatment of Adults with Community-acquired
Pneumonia
An Of cial Clinical Practice Guideline of the American Thoracic Society and
Infectious Diseases Society of America: Executive Summary
Joshua P. Metlay*, Grant W. Waterer*, Ann C. Long, Antonio Anzueto, Jan Brozek, Kristina Crothers, Laura A. Cooley,
Nathan C. Dean, Michael J. Fine, Scott A. Flanders, Marie R. Grif n, Mark L. Metersky, Daniel M. Musher,
Marcos I. Restrepo, and Cynthia G. Whitney; on behalf of the American Thoracic Society and Infectious Diseases
Society of America
T A T S M 2019 I D S A
HIS OFFICIAL CLINICAL PRACTICE GUIDELINE WAS APPROVED BY THE MERICAN HORACIC OCIETY AY AND THE NFECTIOUS ISEASES OCIETY OF MERICA
UGUST
Background: This document provides evidence-based clinical decisions. Although some recommendations remain
practice guidelines on the management of adult patients with unchanged from the 2007 guideline, the availability of
community-acquired pneumonia. results from new therapeutic trials and epidemiological
investigations led to revised recommendations for
Methods: A multidisciplinary panel conducted pragmatic
empiric treatment strategies and additional management
systematic reviews of the relevant research and applied Grading of
decisions.
Recommendations, Assessment, Development, and Evaluation
methodology for clinical recommendations. Conclusions: The panel formulated and provided the rationale
for recommendations on selected diagnostic and treatment
Results: The panel addressed 16 speci c areas for
strategies for adult patients with community-acquired pneumonia.
recommendations spanning questions of diagnostic
testing, determination of site of care, selection of initial Keywords: community-acquired pneumonia; pneumonia; patient
empiric antibiotic therapy, and subsequent management management
*Co–first authors.
Endorsed by the Society of Infectious Disease Pharmacists July 2019.
Supported by the American Thoracic Society and Infectious Diseases Society of America.
The findings and conclusions in this report are those of the authors and do not necessarily represent the official position of the U.S. CDC.
This Executive Summary is part of the full official ATS clinical practice guideline, which readers may access online at http://www.atsjournals.org/
doi/abs/10.1164/rccm.201908-1581ST. Only the Executive Summary is appearing in the print edition of the Journal. The article of record, and the
one that should be cited, is: Diagnosis and Treatment of Adults with Community-acquired Pneumonia: An Official Clinical Practice Guideline of the
American Thoracic Society and Infectious Diseases Society of America. Am J Respir Crit Care Med 2019;200:e45–e67.
Youmay printone copyof thisdocumentat nocharge. However,ifyourequire morethanone copy,youmustplaceareprint order.Domestic reprint orders:
amy.schriver@sheridan.com; international reprint orders: louisa.mott@springer.com.
This article has an online supplement, which is accessible from this issue’s table of contents at www.atsjournals.org.
Am J Respir Crit Care Med Vol 200, Iss 7, pp 809–821, Oct 1, 2019

Internet address: www.atsjournals.org
